113674.explanation: Quality and commitment in Enkhuizen come first. Diversity, including good representation of women, is important, but a fixed quota should not be leading.

113675.explanation: Residents must be able to voice their opinions on important issues, but a referendum is a supplement to the council and requires clear rules and good information.

113678.explanation: Additional activities at a farm can offer opportunities but are not suitable everywhere. The municipality must assess per situation what is spatially, traffic-wise, and environmentally responsible.

113682.explanation: Employers have a responsibility for proper housing of seasonal workers, but this does not always have to be on their own property. The municipality should allow room for customized solutions that fit the environment, livability, and regulations.

113684.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves may be used with attention to health and nuisance. Conscious burning, good information, and enforcement in case of nuisance are important.

113689.explanation: Accessibility is important, but the responsibility primarily lies with entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality can lead by example in its own buildings and encourage with knowledge and advice, not structural subsidies.

113691.explanation: Sports and exercise are important for health and social participation. Existing schemes can support people who cannot afford membership themselves, so money is not a barrier to sports.

113694.explanation: Music education is valuable, but it is not necessary to subsidize it for every child. The municipality can stimulate music education through schools and existing programs, with extra attention for children who would otherwise not have access.

113696.explanation: Monuments are important for the identity and appeal of Enkhuizen. Proper maintenance prevents higher costs in the future and contributes to livability, tourism, and preserving the city's historic character.

113698.explanation: In specific locations with persistent safety problems, camera surveillance can be an appropriate measure. This is done purposefully and temporarily, after a decision by the public prosecutor, considering privacy and as a supplement to supervision and prevention.

113702.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation and tourism. Permanent residence puts pressure on facilities and enforcement and undermines the recreational character. Housing needs are solved with real, suitable homes.

113703.explanation: New construction should take place as much as possible within the existing city boundaries. This protects the open landscape, better utilizes space, and keeps Enkhuizen compact and livable, with attention to quality and appropriate housing.

113704.explanation: Participation in society is important but should not be mandatory. We focus on guidance, activation, and suitable work from a position of trust, taking into account a person's situation and capabilities.

113706.explanation: The current income limits do not always match reality. By raising the limit, we also reach people just above minimum income who still struggle to make ends meet.

113708.explanation: Good charging infrastructure is necessary for the transition to electric driving. By making more parking spaces suitable for charging, we support this development, with attention to fair distribution of public space.

113709.explanation: 30 km/h zones can contribute to traffic safety and livability on some streets but are not appropriate everywhere. We choose tailored solutions per street, depending on function, layout, and traffic volume.

113710.explanation: Good care is person-centered. Where possible, people should be able to choose a care provider who matches their faith, culture, or beliefs, within the boundaries of quality, availability, and affordability.

113712.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is necessary to maintain facilities. We do not choose a general reduction but look specifically at affordability and smart spending choices to keep burdens and services balanced.

113714.explanation: The tourist tax was recently increased. Another increase is not appropriate now because tourism is important for Enkhuizen and contributes to vibrancy and economic vitality.

113715.explanation: Business parks must provide space for local entrepreneurs to grow and become sustainable. Expansion is possible if carefully integrated, fitting the scale of Enkhuizen and with attention to livability and landscape.

113717.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce. We choose a fair distribution where urgency counts but no groups are structurally favored. We focus on sufficient housing construction so everyone has perspective.

113719.explanation: Solar panels should primarily be placed on roofs and existing paved areas. Agricultural land is scarce and important for landscape and food production. Only in exceptional cases can customized solutions be considered.

113720.explanation: Cinema Enkhuizen is an important cultural facility and meeting place. Municipal support helps maintain a diverse and accessible film offering and contributes to the city's vibrancy.

113724.explanation: A fixed share of 40% social rental housing does not adequately reflect the local situation. Enkhuizen already has a high share of social rental housing. By managing each location for a good housing mix, more choice, quality, and flow in the housing market are created.

113725.explanation: High residential towers do not suit the character and scale of Enkhuizen. We choose housing that fits the surroundings and contributes to livability, with attention to quality and integration into the city.

113727.explanation: More parking spaces in the center come at the expense of space for housing, greenery, and recreation. We focus on parking at the edges of the city and good accessibility to keep the center livable and attractive.

113729.explanation: A mandatory registration for all boats is not necessary. We choose clear rules and enforcement where needed, without extra administrative burdens for the municipality and boat owners.

113731.explanation: A municipal merger is not an end in itself. First, we must get our own organization and finances in order. From a strong and independent position, it can then be considered whether closer cooperation or merging adds value.

113732.explanation: A nighttime ban on electric bicycles goes too far. We opt for clear rules, proper behavior, and enforcement in case of danger or nuisance, so pedestrians are safe without unnecessary prohibitions.

113733.explanation: More public toilets increase the accessibility and livability of the city center. They are important for the elderly, families, and visitors and contribute to a welcoming and attractive city.

113674.explanation: Agreed. Unfortunately, the parties do not decide on the newly to be proposed aldermen. Sadly, politics is still predominantly male, so the choice of men is larger than that of women.

113675.explanation: The SP has advocated this for years. The establishment of citizens' assemblies, which have increasingly proven effective, is an interim solution to restore residents’ trust in politics and give them more say. Referenda can complement this.

113678.explanation: Farmers are struggling and support where possible is desirable. This should only concern farmers who farm independently and are not employed by large 'lord' farmers.

113682.explanation: The Roemer report advised that seasonal workers should not depend on their employer for housing. A central housing location for seasonal workers, like in Zeewolde, with sufficient facilities is a good solution.

113684.explanation: There are residents with limited budgets who reduce their energy bills by using wood stoves. However, residents should be considerate by avoiding burning during foggy or windless conditions. One option could be to designate part of a new neighborhood as wood stove-free.

113689.explanation: The surroundings are the municipality’s responsibility. Access to the entrepreneur’s own building is their own responsibility. In exceptional cases, the municipality can consider advising.

113691.explanation: The participation fund was set up for this and the municipality also subsidizes Stichting Leergeld, which provides support here. But again, more tailored solutions can resolve more issues.

113694.explanation: Without an introduction, no talent development. Every child should have the opportunity to get acquainted with art and culture. It’s about more than just music lessons. This introduction should be subsidized by the municipality. If a child is talented, it is up to the parents to support them further.

113696.explanation: Monuments tell our history; we borrow them from our ancestors and must pass them on to the next generation. Municipalities with many monuments should be supported by the national government. History is important for all of the Netherlands, for us and tourists.

113698.explanation: Camera surveillance is a last resort and creates a displacement effect. The community officers should be more visible on the streets. They are the hosts of our city. Focusing on preventing nuisance is much more important and requires mainly listening to our residents.

113702.explanation: As a temporary measure to solve the urgent housing shortage, this must be a temporary option. There are too many people without a roof over their heads or in dire living situations.

113703.explanation: Yes, because the housing shortage is severe and building within the city is very expensive. As long as residents wait years on social housing lists, building must continue.

113704.explanation: Security of livelihood is a right. Here too, tailored solutions are the answer. The best way out of poverty is paid work. We assist social welfare recipients as much as possible towards paid work, meaningful volunteer work, or proper daytime activities. According to everyone’s abilities and tailored to their needs. If it doesn’t work out, there is no obligation.

113706.explanation: Support and preventive measures are very important. Tailoring should be the solution. Raising the income limit will prevent problems and ultimately more expensive solutions. Scientific research repeatedly shows that every euro invested yields multiple euros in return.

113708.explanation: Charging stations for electric cars must be arranged so that cars not charging do not occupy the spots. Thus, the spots are only available for charging. We focus on innovation, for example by turning streetlights into charging stations.

113709.explanation: Main roads should remain at 50 km/h. A mobile speed camera can be used to temper speed. On other roads, the layout should encourage driving at 30 km/h. But a change in mentality to safely share the roads together is also necessary.

113710.explanation: There are major shortages in healthcare. If possible, matching based on faith, culture, or beliefs is fine, but it may not be a requirement. Given the current labor market situation, we cannot afford that.

113712.explanation: In principle, only increase by the inflation correction percentage. But in recent years it was raised due to reductions in funds from the national government, and last year because of green maintenance. The property tax increase is a fraction of the annual rent increase that tenants face.

113714.explanation: We increase the tourist tax by the inflation correction percentage, but also look at other municipalities in the region to stay aligned with them.

113715.explanation: Within current zoning plans. There are still free plots, and Enkhuizen has no more land available.

113717.explanation: Refugees are not able to register in time. The housing shortage is not the refugees’ fault but due to poor national policy. Social housing is being sold off and not enough is built. There must be 40% social housing so everyone has a home.

113719.explanation: First solar panels on roofs. There are still enough suitable roofs without solar panels. That takes priority. Parking lots can also still be covered with solar panels.

113720.explanation: Cinema Enkhuizen, like any other association or club, has the right to apply for a subsidy, provided it fits within the subsidy policy and brings social value.

113724.explanation: In Enkhuizen, the proportion of social rental housing is also 28%. The waiting time for social housing is 11.51 years, and the most immediate relief of housing shortage is by building at least 40% social housing. We build based on need; housing is a right, not a profit model.

113725.explanation: But only along the edges of the city. And a maximum of 5 residential floors.

113727.explanation: We park at the edges of the city. Parking spaces that exist in the city center are reserved for residents. Parking spaces for disabled or less mobile people remain in the city.

113729.explanation: Nuisance on the water is significant. Enforcement is very complicated; registration with license plates can help remove offenders from anonymity. Water recreation should be pleasant for everyone. We will consult residents to see if they want to help develop solutions.

113731.explanation: Enkhuizen remains independent for now. Our social policy is more social than that of neighboring municipalities. We already have an administrative merger. Residents should be able to express their opinion about a political merger via citizens’ assemblies or referendum.

113732.explanation: When stores are open, people should walk. When stores are closed, cycling is allowed, whether electric or regular — it makes no difference.

113733.explanation: There is a shortage of public toilets. The emergency toilet app offers a partial solution but not outside opening hours. Then enough public toilets must be accessible. Expanding public toilets in the inner and outer city also benefits businesses.

113674.explanation: CDA Enkhuizen values a good reflection of society in the council and the municipal executive. However, that does not mean the number of men and women in the executive must be equal. Leaders should above all be chosen based on quality, not gender.

113675.explanation: With a referendum, you can only answer 'yes' or 'no' to complex issues. This causes decisions to be made purely on emotion instead of facts and nuance. We advocate for more citizen involvement before policies are set, such as through a citizens’ panel.

113678.explanation: CDA branches generally support allowing a store or campsite at a farm. This helps farmers earn extra income and strengthens the local economy. It must fit the landscape, zoning plans, and be coordinated with local residents.

113682.explanation: West Frisian municipalities should encourage agricultural employers as much as possible to arrange housing for seasonal workers themselves. It’s a win-win: it reduces nuisance for our residents and frees up homes in residential neighborhoods for our residents.

113684.explanation: Municipalities cannot ban the use of fireplaces and wood stoves. They are allowed products on the European market. However, CDA Enkhuizen supports regulating their use to minimize air pollution as much as possible.

113689.explanation: For CDA Enkhuizen, it is clear: everyone who can, participates. In Enkhuizen, no one is left aside. Not only the municipality but society as a whole is responsible for ensuring everyone can participate. Entrepreneurs also bear responsibility in this.

113691.explanation: CDA Enkhuizen wants to provide sports passes to children from less well-off families. This makes sports more accessible for them.

113694.explanation: Music and culture connect people and encourage personal development. CDA Enkhuizen wants to make culture accessible to everyone. However, this does not mean every primary school child needs a subsidy. We therefore look at tailored ways to stimulate children from less affluent backgrounds.

113696.explanation: Enkhuizen breathes monuments. From the Drommedaris to the Zuiderkerk. We want to share this culture with everyone. That is why we have invested a lot of money in the maintenance of monuments over the past years. As far as we are concerned, there should be no cuts here, but also no additional spending.

113698.explanation: More camera surveillance in public spaces contributes to prevention and increases the chance of catching offenders. At the request of entrepreneurs, CDA Enkhuizen wants to expand camera surveillance in Schepenwijk and Krabbersplaat. This helps tackle crime and nuisance.

113702.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in holiday homes is not a sustainable solution to the large housing shortage. Holiday homes are not designed for permanent occupancy. The real solution lies in building more homes and making better use of the existing housing stock.

113703.explanation: CDA generally supports allowing a little extra street. One of the causes of the large housing shortage is that new construction outside the existing village boundaries has been delayed for too long, among other things by the province.

113704.explanation: Socially meaningful work provides structure, purpose, and social contacts. This keeps people involved and counteracts loneliness. Therefore, we want to encourage people on welfare to do such work as much as possible.

113706.explanation: The income threshold for receiving poverty assistance in Enkhuizen is already higher (120%) than in Stede Broec and Drechterland (110%). Further increasing this would attract people from neighboring municipalities and raise costs for residents. This type of aid remains intended for those who truly need it.

113708.explanation: The use of electric cars will continue to increase in the coming years. That simply means more charging points need to be added. CDA Enkhuizen especially wants to focus on the edges of the city center to minimize the risk of major damage from fires.

113709.explanation: The public road must be safe and accessible for everyone. Traffic is becoming busier. This increases the chance of accidents. A lower maximum speed improves traffic safety.

113710.explanation: Care that aligns with a person’s faith or culture increases the sense of safety and trust. Without this freedom of choice, some groups may avoid care or only seek help later. This leads to unnecessary health disparities between groups.

113712.explanation: CDA Enkhuizen has opposed large increases in property tax (ozb) for years. For example, entrepreneurs in Enkhuizen already pay 56% more ozb than in all other West Frisian municipalities. Our position is clear: we will not let the ozb rise above inflation. We will first look at savings within the municipality itself.

113714.explanation: As far as CDA Enkhuizen is concerned, tourism is not a side issue. We want to invest in the coming years to make Enkhuizen even more attractive to tourists. This can be partly paid for by increasing the tourist tax. Tourists would then directly contribute to better and more facilities.

113715.explanation: We continue with the expansion of Schepenwijk 2.0. This gives companies space to grow, with less focus on small units. In addition, we will expand camera surveillance in Schepenwijk and Krabbersplaat. This creates more jobs and income for our city.

113717.explanation: The housing of status holders is determined nationally through a quota set by the government. Municipalities are not obliged to give extra priority to social rental housing. In West Friesland, about 6% of homes already go to status holders; further priority increases pressure on other housing seekers.

113719.explanation: For CDA, solar panels on agricultural land are the very last option. We find it a terrible waste to place solar farms on fertile soil. Generally, there is also no local support for this. Solar panels on roofs and above parking lots are good alternatives.

113720.explanation: The municipality should work with De Drom to see how the cinema can be made profitable. If this is not possible, we want to realize a profitable cinema together with the post office in Bovenkarspel. This would allow De Drom to rent out two additional halls, enabling it to generate more income itself.

113724.explanation: 40% social rental housing is not achievable or affordable. Housing corporations lose an average of 48 euros per home per month. CDA Enkhuizen wants 30% social rental; 35% affordable owner-occupied and mid-range rent; and 35% higher-priced in new neighborhoods. This creates a good mix of homes for various target groups.

113725.explanation: CDA Enkhuizen also wants to realize residential flats in our new neighborhoods Gouwe Vaart and Nijevoert. Forty percent of active housing seekers in this region prefer to live in an apartment. Building apartments is also necessary to keep housing projects affordable.

113727.explanation: Parking pressure will increase in the coming years, possibly requiring more parking spaces. Where possible, we will therefore look for locations to create additional parking. We do recognize this is challenging.

113729.explanation: Boats over 20hp must already be registered. Additional registration is therefore unnecessary. Besides, we lack the enforcement capacity for this.

113731.explanation: In principle, CDA Enkhuizen supports a administrative merger with Stede Broec and Drechterland. It leads to more efficient work, lower costs, and better services. Conditions are that residents support it, neighboring municipalities participate, and Enkhuizen retains its identity.

113732.explanation: Electric bicycles often ride faster and more quietly. Pedestrians are less alert in the dark. This can pose additional danger at night. Moreover, a single rule for day and night prevents confusion.

113733.explanation: A tourist-friendly city should have enough public toilets. Fortunately, toilet locations in the city hall and harbor are already public and findable via the app. Additionally, we think the municipality should encourage entrepreneurs to make their toilets easy to find.

113674.explanation: This is what we strive for.

113691.explanation: That does not seem financially feasible.

113706.explanation: The municipality must adhere to the national standard for the social minimum.

113712.explanation: Depends on the financial condition of the municipality.

113725.explanation: The question is how high and where.

113674.explanation: Diversity in the executive board is a nice ambition, but the most important thing is that the board consists of people fully committed to the city.

113675.explanation: Participation is of course important. We believe it is already sufficiently arranged through elections and the possibility to participate in municipal council meetings.

113682.explanation: Seasonal workers must be housed decently. This can be done in various ways. It is important to prevent abuses.

113684.explanation: We choose to encourage residents to heat their homes sustainably.

113689.explanation: We want everyone to be able to participate in society. If such initiatives contribute to this, we support them.

113691.explanation: This is existing policy and we will continue with it.

113694.explanation: We are not in favor of a general subsidy for music lessons. We do see opportunities to collaborate with various associations to set up initiatives in education, sports, and culture.

113696.explanation: A large part of this is paid by the national government. Moreover, monuments are part of our city and we must cherish them.

113698.explanation: The balance between privacy and security is extremely careful. This requires a tailored approach.

113702.explanation: Holiday parks are not suitable for permanent residence.

113703.explanation: The new residential neighborhoods Gouwe Vaart and Nijevoert will be developed outside the village boundaries. We continue to support these projects.

113704.explanation: We encourage this, but we do not want to make it mandatory.

113706.explanation: We want to maintain the current policy and certainly not lower the income threshold. Poverty policy is a priority for our party.

113708.explanation: We support the energy transition. As a municipality, we will need to respond to this.

113709.explanation: For safety and environmental reasons, we agree with this statement.

113710.explanation: We agree with this statement, but have doubts about the municipality's influence on this matter.

113712.explanation: Given the municipality's financial situation, we do not think it is wise to lower the property tax (ozb).

113714.explanation: The tourist tax has recently been increased. We do not rule out changes in the future.

113715.explanation: At this moment, we see no need to expand business parks.

113717.explanation: We adhere to the current policy on this.

113719.explanation: This depends on the situation. We first focus on solar panels on roofs; only after that will we look for alternative locations.

113720.explanation: We must maintain the facilities in Enkhuizen. We hold the Drom and the Cinema close to our hearts.

113724.explanation: We strive for 30% social rental housing, 40% affordable owner-occupied housing, and 30% private sector housing.

113725.explanation: With the current housing shortage, this is unavoidable. We find it important that this is well integrated with plenty of public green space.

113727.explanation: In our view, the goal of the parking policy is a city center with less car traffic.

113729.explanation: Boat owners already pay harbor fees. Registering the boats adds no value.

113731.explanation: We are in favor of an administrative merger between Enkhuizen, Stede Broec, and Drechterland.

113732.explanation: The current policy suffices. Moreover, this ban is unenforceable.

113733.explanation: The lack of public toilets is a major problem in the Netherlands. We must commit ourselves to public toilets for residents and visitors.

113674.explanation: HEA aims to achieve a proper representation of women and men in all layers of society, including in a college of Mayor and Aldermen. For the 2026 elections, HEA has 2 women in the top 3 positions and 4 women among the first 9 candidates.

113675.explanation: HEA strives for optimal transparency and openness in politics. On important issues, a referendum could be a good instrument to increase citizen participation. Participation instead of merely informing.

113678.explanation: It is logical that a municipality assesses whether a campsite or shop can be established next to a farm and grants a permit accordingly, but it should not prohibit or allow this in advance.

113682.explanation: Not necessarily, however there is a housing shortage. Using housing for seasonal workers on the company's own site will not increase pressure on the housing market. Housing seasonal workers is more a shared responsibility between employer and government.

113684.explanation: There should be a well-considered and acceptable policy on burning from the municipality, setting out when burning is recommended or not.

113689.explanation: Laws and regulations are leading in this; for historical buildings in Enkhuizen, it is difficult to make a building more accessible. Consultation is important to discuss the possibilities and limitations and to find solutions. Consult with each other and reach acceptable solutions.

113691.explanation: Exercise is essential for the health of young and old. When financial constraints prevent healthy sports participation, the local government should reasonably provide support.

113694.explanation: It would be a better alternative if primary school children received music lessons as part of the curriculum at school.

113696.explanation: HEA sees heritage not as a burden, but as an asset. Monuments and historical structures contribute to identity, tourism, and livability of Enkhuizen. Therefore, our heritage deserves an active and future-proof policy. Heritage must be maintained with respect and care.

113698.explanation: CCTV surveillance is one of the most obvious options to achieve a safer city and environment. CCTV is one of the most intrusive forms of surveillance regarding privacy. There must be a good balance between deployment/purpose and privacy protection.

113702.explanation: Provided laws and regulations do not oppose this. Otherwise, adjust these laws/regulations at the local municipal level, considering the scarcity and tightness of the housing market.

113703.explanation: Building is necessary and can also take place outside existing village boundaries, provided laws and regulations allow it.

113704.explanation: To promote social cohesion, inclusion, and self-worth through active participation in society, it would be desirable if this were possible.

113706.explanation: Agreed, but increases also mean extra costs; the pie does not get bigger but the number of consumers does. We can better focus on prevention/education/awareness as means to address (the expansion of) poverty. It is a combined issue.

113708.explanation: Efficient use of space in and around the city by creating combinations of living, parking, and working. Encouraging initiatives by entrepreneurs on existing industrial sites regarding parking, environment, and sustainability. In short, developing a broad integrated parking policy.

113709.explanation: Certainly in terms of safety, children participating in traffic, traffic volume/speed in a historic narrow city center. But when lowering the maximum speed, speeding violations must be enforced.

113710.explanation: The principle is that everyone who needs care can rely on care providers and express a preference for a provider that suits them. Given scarcity, this may not always be honored.

113712.explanation: The property tax (OZB) has increased too much in Enkhuizen in recent years and is seen by residents as a milking cow that is repeatedly used to raise revenue in case of (possible) deficits.

113714.explanation: Annual increase, in addition to indexation; the increase must benefit tourism and the beautification of the historic city center and harbors.

113715.explanation: When there is sufficient land available for expansion, this is certainly an option. Expansion should also focus on making industrial areas more sustainable.

113717.explanation: Everyone with a housing desire who is registered as a housing seeker must be treated equally.

113719.explanation: The preference is for roofs and other forms of sustainability regarding the energy transition. Solar meadows often come at the expense of available and valuable agricultural land.

113720.explanation: Culture ensures that people meet each other and feel at home in their city, whether it's a performance in the cinema, exhibitions in the Zuiderzeemuseum, a music evening in the city center, or a creative activity in the community center. Therefore, HEA wants culture to be accessible to everyone.

113724.explanation: Enkhuizen is a municipality with a relatively large group of people eligible for social rental housing; however, 40% is a high number compared to the national average.

113725.explanation: Given the historic character of the city and its many monuments/skyline, flats that are higher than the existing ones do not seem desirable.

113727.explanation: Expansion of parking capacity, for example at Ossenmarkt, by realizing multiple parking decks. Use locations such as the Flevoland terrain as long-term parking spots for cars. Encourage initiatives by entrepreneurs on existing industrial sites regarding parking (Mhub).

113729.explanation: This is related to enforcement in areas outside the regular harbors.

113731.explanation: HEA is in favor of an administrative merger of the participating municipalities within the SED. Besides a strong, fully-fledged municipality that can work more efficiently/effectively, the dynamic power balance toward the region/province changes. Merger based on equality and distinctiveness.

113732.explanation: A pedestrian zone is a pedestrian zone; deviations in accessibility lead to confusion, uncertainty, and possibly dangerous situations, both during the day and at night.

113733.explanation: Indeed, more public toilets that are accessible to people with disabilities, well-maintained hygienically, and can be seen as a service to residents and tourists shopping in the city.

113674.explanation: We believe that the executive board should be filled with people who have expertise. Preference based on gender should never be a specific selection criterion!

113675.explanation: We have a double stance here; of course, the citizen must be heard. But if everything becomes part of a referendum, our city becomes ungovernable.

113678.explanation: As an entrepreneurs' party, we certainly applaud this!

113682.explanation: We believe this should be done as much as possible; this frees up housing for our own residents. Also, better control over the living conditions of seasonal workers is possible here.

113684.explanation: It is a utopian thought that the use of stoves or open fireplaces can be abolished. We do believe that municipalities can take a more advisory role, for example, providing information when it is better not to burn due to weather conditions.

113689.explanation: Although we understand this point, it is certainly something that lies with the entrepreneur and not the municipality.

113691.explanation: Although we understand the desire, we still believe that the responsibility lies with the individual. Such a scheme exists for children, but for adults, I think there are enough options to meet with various clubs.

113694.explanation: Music is an important part of a child's upbringing. The municipality should certainly facilitate this.

113696.explanation: Enkhuizen is a city with a rich history and therefore many monuments. Cutting back here will result in higher costs in the long term. However, we believe that we must be critical of what is done.

113698.explanation: It is extremely important for residents to reduce nuisance. Camera surveillance at nuisance locations such as Koperwiekplein but also at Drecht, Schepenwijk, or Krabbersplaat is important to us for both residents and entrepreneurs.

113702.explanation: A holiday home is not intended for permanent residence.

113703.explanation: In the current market, we will also have to build beyond the boundaries to ensure that housing remains accessible in the future. However, we must critically assess whether a plan fits the location.

113704.explanation: We believe it is important that people with benefits who can work do socially valuable work. Even more important is that these people find their way to regular paid employment. Social work can certainly contribute to this.

113706.explanation: As harsh as it sounds, the answer is no. We already have an increased limit. We believe that help should be provided where possible, but this also depends on the person.

113708.explanation: Given the net congestion we now see, we do not believe that additional charging points will help. However, we do believe the municipality should investigate how we can better facilitate cars being charged at the doorstep.

113709.explanation: A reduction of the maximum speed limit is non-negotiable for us because it is simply unenforceable. It seems better to look at the road design to reduce speed where necessary.

113712.explanation: Property tax (OZB) has become a cash cow for the current coalition in Enkhuizen over recent years. Instead of critically reviewing expenditures, OZB has been used like an ATM.

113714.explanation: This has already been increased recently. As the VVD, we believe we first need to wait for the effects of this.

113715.explanation: To facilitate our entrepreneurs and secure local employment, this is certainly important to us!

113717.explanation: Although this is legally regulated, we believe that local residents should indeed be given priority.

113719.explanation: There are many options to place solar panels, such as parking lots and roofs of businesses. Sacrificing agricultural land for this is, in our view, not the way.

113720.explanation: Cinema Enkhuizen is free to apply for subsidies.

113724.explanation: We will only achieve flow in the housing market if we build fewer social rental homes and focus more on affordable owner-occupied homes to advance the housing career; clear agreements must be made about this.

113725.explanation: At the edges of Enkhuizen, such as the Gouw, along the railway line, and the Haling, it should be possible to build higher layers. This will provide more homes for our residents.

113727.explanation: There should be more parking spaces at the edge of the city center, places we have asked for a long time but the current college does not prioritize much. More spots should be created in the neighborhoods outside the center where parking pressure is very high.

113729.explanation: To reduce nuisance and facilitate enforcement, this would be a good step.

113731.explanation: Enkhuizen will have to merge in the future; it is simply not feasible for a municipality of our size to continue alone. The current SED organization was set up to increase cooperation and eventually become a municipality. The challenges are becoming too big and costly.

113732.explanation: In our view, this could only apply if it concerns all bicycles, not just electric ones.

113733.explanation: This would certainly be an important step for a city center like ours.

113674.explanation: Equal opportunities for everyone. We want the best and/or most suitable. We know this is independent of gender. With equal opportunities, being a woman can be an advantage.

113675.explanation: Currently, there is much apparent participation. Only by giving citizens direct influence will things change.

113678.explanation: Improve small scale and the local economy.

113682.explanation: Many more sectors use labor migrants. Why make distinctions? If a farmer provides housing themselves, they will receive more offers from workers.

113689.explanation: There are already requirements. No need to allocate public funds for this.

113691.explanation: The idea is nice. The municipality could also pay part or introduce a minimum pass. We will look into this!

113696.explanation: If the economy falters, the municipality must also take a step back.

113698.explanation: Cameras mainly shift the problem. More enforcement!

113702.explanation: Disagree. But NOT enforcement. People are often here out of necessity!

113703.explanation: Enkhuizen has no further space. However, prevent everything from becoming grey. Lots of greenery!

113706.explanation: Inflation only makes the problem worse.

113710.explanation: Care should be neutral regarding religion, culture, and beliefs. Of course, this does not mean these issues cannot be discussed.

113712.explanation: Increased year after year. Despite the coalition's promise not to do so. The municipality should first manage its own costs instead of constantly passing the bill to the citizens!

113715.explanation: Could be increased a bit! Entrepreneurs also create jobs. Crucial for a small municipality.

113717.explanation: Once a refugee has a residence permit, those people should be placed in the same waiting queue as the local population. No 'positive' discrimination either.

113720.explanation: Money was given last year to get everything in order. In 2 years we will review how it goes.

113725.explanation: Huge demand for housing. If an additional layer can be added, for example near the railway (Nijevoort) or in Gommer West-West, where high-rise is planned, consider adding an extra layer, provided the environment allows it.

113727.explanation: But not less! Parking for tourists outside the center!

113729.explanation: More enforcement instead of trying to solve the problem through administration. Because enforcement is needed then too!

113731.explanation: Only increases overhead and distance from the citizen.

113733.explanation: Currently brushed off with a mobile app, but the municipality should show courtesy here. Especially considering the recently increased tourist tax! Citizens can expect something in return!

113674.explanation: Enkhuizen needs aldermen who are competent; gender does not matter for SGP Enkhuizen.

113675.explanation: It can be helpful, but it depends on the type of referendum. It must also be very clear beforehand what the purpose of the referendum is and how the outcome will be involved in the decision-making process.

113678.explanation: Where possible, we must give farmers space to do something extra at the farm. Usually this is a small shop, very rarely a campsite. Municipal policy determines under which conditions something is possible or not.

113682.explanation: In West Friesland we already have experience housing seasonal workers on the farm; that goes well. We are also fine with a horticulturist housing workers at a fellow horticulturist.

113684.explanation: We take nuisance very seriously. However, a total ban is not realistic. It is necessary to take measures that limit nuisance and promote responsible behavior when burning.

113689.explanation: It is good if as many buildings as possible are accessible to people with disabilities. The municipality arranges this for its buildings, entrepreneurs for their own buildings. Everyone has their own responsibility in this.

113691.explanation: Children must not suffer from their parents' financial situation.

113694.explanation: We already help families who cannot afford music lessons for their children. Subsidies are not necessary for all primary school children.

113696.explanation: The SGP is proud of Enkhuizen's monuments. We must cherish and take good care of this heritage so future generations can be proud of it as well.

113698.explanation: At locations with repeated nuisance, a camera can sometimes be a solution. Important to know: strict requirements apply to the use of camera surveillance; a 'feeling of insecurity' is not valid justification. A municipality may not just apply camera surveillance at will.

113702.explanation: A holiday park is not suitable as a residential area. Enkhuizen needs good residential neighborhoods.

113703.explanation: We like to make use of a suitable place to build, even if it is outside the built-up area.

113704.explanation: Residents who receive benefits may be expected to provide a 'counter-performance'. This can for example take the form of volunteer work or a work experience placement, and should be focused as much as possible on active participation.

113706.explanation: There are always families who just fall outside the income limit. We choose the solution to offer those families a percentage of the assistance.

113708.explanation: We support innovative solutions to safely bring the charging cable via the sidewalk to the car. Parking spaces must be available for both electric and non-electric cars. On larger sites, such as at the NS station, there is room to reserve spots as charging points.

113709.explanation: Most roads are already limited to 30 km/h. At the end of last year, the municipal council adopted a new traffic plan that lowers the speed on some roads from 50 to 30 km/h. However, a number of roads are perfectly suitable for a maximum speed of 50 km/h; no adjustment is needed there.

113710.explanation: If you need care, you want to be helped in a way that suits you. The government should not simply restrict that freedom.

113712.explanation: If we arrange things more efficiently in Enkhuizen, less money will be wasted and the property tax (OZB) can be lowered.

113714.explanation: We must prevent Enkhuizen from becoming much more expensive than the surrounding municipalities. In the future, an increase in tourist tax is conceivable. This will partly depend on the choices of other municipalities in the region.

113715.explanation: Entrepreneurs in small and medium-sized enterprises are the backbone of our economy. If expansion is possible, the SGP supports it.

113717.explanation: We want to prioritize people who urgently need a home, for example in cases of acute family disruption where children are in danger.

113719.explanation: We use agricultural land for food supply.

113720.explanation: We only subsidize Cinema Enkhuizen when they comply with the subsidy scheme.

113724.explanation: For us, the location determines the type of homes that will be built there. Seniors often have a home that has become too large. For them, we want solid apartments in the best locations of Enkhuizen. We do not consider social rental homes wise in those locations.

113725.explanation: There are locations in Enkhuizen where you can well build somewhat taller residential complexes, just look at the apartments at Koperwiek or Paktuinen. Really much taller residential towers do not fit with Enkhuizen.

113727.explanation: But not at the expense of public green spaces.

113729.explanation: Boat registration is not needed to enforce regulations. If enforcement is better organized, problems will decrease.

113731.explanation: The administration must be close to the residents: Enkhuizen residents decide what happens in Enkhuizen. Research also shows that municipal mergers rarely result in cost savings.

113732.explanation: Cycling outside store opening hours is fine on Westerstraat. During opening hours, enforcement should be improved so cycling is not allowed at that time.

113733.explanation: There are already quite a number of locations in Enkhuizen, visible via an app, where visitors can find toilets. Realizing a public toilet by the municipality often costs (much) more than 60,000 euros.

statements.title: Enkhuizen should merge with another municipality.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain allowed in Enkhuizen.

titleParts.text: In Enkhuizen moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Enkhuizen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The pedestrian area should also be off-limits to electric bicycles at night.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The residents of Enkhuizen should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Enkhuizen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: More tall residential blocks should be allowed in Enkhuizen.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
Owners should be required to register their boat with the municipality.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Industrial/business parks in Enkhuizen should be allowed to expand.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Enkhuizen should stop providing funding to Cinema Enkhuizen.
Of the newly built homes in Enkhuizen, at least 40% should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Enkhuizen moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality of Enkhuizen should provide more public toilets in the town center.
